text: In this research, we introduce BEVDepth, a high-performance 3D object detector designed for camera-based Bird’s-Eye-View (BEV) perception. Our approach is motivated by the observation that while depth is a fundamental component of camera-based 3D detection, depth estimation in existing methods remains surprisingly inadequate. BEVDepth addresses this deficiency by incorporating explicit depth supervision during training and introducing a camera-aware depth estimation module to enhance predictive accuracy. Additionally, we design a novel Depth Refinement Module to mitigate the spatial errors introduced by imprecise feature unprojection. When integrated with a customized Efficient Voxel Pooling strategy and a multi-frame fusion mechanism, BEVDepth sets a new benchmark on the challenging nuScenes test set with 60.9% NDS. Notably, this represents the first time a camera-only model has surpassed the 60% NDS threshold while maintaining high computational efficiency. Code and models have been made publicly available.
